commands.review_summary:
The PR adds a macOS memory-pressure monitor that evicts idle OpenAI dashboard WebViews and calls malloc pressure relief, plus an idle-eviction cache API and focused cache coverage.

Reproducibility: not applicable. as a feature/performance PR. Source inspection confirms current main lacks a memory-pressure eviction trigger, but the PR still needs after-fix runtime proof of the new path.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] Needs contributor after-fix real behavior proof and normal maintainer review; automation cannot produce the contributor’s memory-pressure runtime evidence.

target.title: Release idle WebViews on memory pressure
target.body:
## Summary

- Add a macOS memory-pressure monitor for warning and critical pressure events.
- Evict only idle cached OpenAI dashboard WebViews when pressure arrives, preserving active dashboard refreshes.
- Run malloc pressure relief alongside the WebView cache cleanup.

## Proof

- Real local before profile, installed CodexBar 0.36.1 with trace logging: main app idle averaged 0.152% CPU and 30.6 MB RSS, while the associated WebKit WebContent process held a 306.1 MB physical footprint with a 541.7 MB peak after dashboard work. Samples were idle/mach_msg dominated, pointing to retained WebKit memory rather than a Swift CPU loop.
- `swift test --filter OpenAIDashboardWebViewCacheTests`: 18 tests passed. New coverage proves idle WebViews are evicted and busy WebViews remain cached.
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftformat Sources Tests --lint`: 0/1140 files require formatting.
- `.build/lint-tools/bin/swiftlint --strict`: 0 violations.
- `swift build --build-tests`: passed.
- `git diff --check`: passed.
- `make check`: locally blocked before Swift lint by the existing `node --check docs/site.js` ESM mismatch under Node v25.9.0; the checked `upstream/main:docs/site.js` starts with an ESM import as well, so this is not introduced by this patch.
target.author: prospectore
